senses.definition: a device that tests and measures hearing by presenting different tones at varying volumes and frequencies

examples.x: The audiometer measured my ability to hear soft tones.
Mary's new hearing aids were adjusted using an audiometer.
The school nurse used an audiometer for student hearing screenings.
The audiometer revealed that the musician had developed some hearing loss over the years.
During my annual checkup, the audiologist used an audiometer to ensure my hearing was still good.

senses.definition: ‌a device used by doctors and physicians that produces an image of structures within the body on a computer screen
a device that spins samples to separate components based on density

examples.x: Medical tests often require the use of a centrifuge.
The clinic acquired a centrifuge for improved sample processing.
Industrial processes employ centrifuges to separate liquids and solids in various substances.
Food industry laboratories use centrifuges for quality control, separating and analyzing ingredients.
Pharmaceutical labs use centrifuges to purify and separate different drug components.

senses.definition: a piece of equipment that uses a preset voltage to apply an electric shock to the chest wall or heart
a device used for controlled and measured release of medications, fluids, or other substances in healthcare settings

examples.x: Hand sanitizer dispensers were placed at various locations.
The automatic pill dispenser helped the elderly manage their medications.
The dental office had a mouthwash dispenser for patient use.
The hand sanitizer dispenser at the entrance promotes infection control.
In the emergency room, a liquid dispenser aided the quick administration of necessary fluids.

senses.definition: a lighted optical instrument that consists of a very small camera and is used to look inside a body cavity or an organ
a device that measures physical work and energy expended during exercise

examples.x: The doctor had me pedal on the bike with an ergometer to check my heart during a test.
After surgery, the therapist used an ergometer to track my strength during exercises.
The treadmill's ergometer at the clinic assessed my fitness level for a health check.
The cardiologist used an ergometer for a stress test to assess my heart's response.
Medical professionals use an ergometer for stress testing during health assessments.

senses.definition: a medical device for holding IV fluid bags and administering fluids or medications to patients
a medical instrument for examining the ear canal and eardrum during medical checkups

examples.x: The doctor checked for ear infections using an otoscope during the visit.
The nurse used an otoscope to examine ears for wax buildup.
The school nurse screened for ear issues with an otoscope during checkups.
Dr. Smith confirmed my ear infection using an otoscope.
During a checkup, an otoscope revealed a foreign object in the ear.

senses.definition: a chair with wheels that is designed particularly for the use of disabled persons

examples.x: He uses a wheelchair to move around the house.
She bought a new wheelchair with an electric motor.
The airport staff helped him board the plane with his wheelchair.
His wheelchair got stuck on the uneven pavement.

senses.definition: a specialized area or station where healthcare professionals perform tasks such as patient monitoring, medication administration, or record-keeping
a flat and thin medical instrument, usually made of wood or plastic, used to hold down the tongue during a mouth and throat examination

examples.x: The doctor used a tongue depressor to examine my throat.
A tongue depressor is commonly used in oral health screenings.
The school nurse grabbed a tongue depressor for a quick throat check.
During the dental exam, the dentist used a tongue depressor.
The pharmacist used a tongue depressor to apply medicine to the back of the throat.

senses.definition: a device used to measure a person's body temperature to assess for fever or abnormal temperature

examples.x: She used a digital thermometer to check her child's temperature when he felt feverish.
The meteorologist used a thermometer to measure the air temperature outside the weather station.
A rectal thermometer is often used for infants and young children to obtain accurate temperature readings.

senses.definition: a device that produces quick flashes of light to make the movements of the vocal cords appear slower for detailed examination and diagnosis

examples.x: Stroboscopes assist in diagnosing vocal cord disorders.
The doctor used a stroboscope for a vocal cord examination.
Medical professionals rely on stroboscopes for vocal cord studies.

senses.definition: a medical device that measures lung air volume during breathing

examples.x: The spirometer results indicated healthy respiratory performance.
The doctor used a spirometer to assess my lung capacity.
A spirometer is a standard tool for assessing lung health in medical examinations.
The respiratory therapist guided the patient in using a spirometer for therapy.
The spirometer helped diagnose respiratory issues by analyzing air volume.

senses.definition: a device with a small wheel and sharp spikes used for sensory stimulation or as a diagnostic tool in neurology
a device that measures blood oxygen saturation non-invasively

examples.x: The nurse used an oximeter to check my blood oxygen level.
Portable oximeters provide real-time oxygen data for patients.
The oximeter reading showed normal blood oxygen during the checkup.
Patients with respiratory issues use oximeters for self-checks.

senses.definition: a device that sterilizes medical equipment using high-pressure steam

examples.x: The autoclave ensured the hygiene of dental tools.
Surgeons placed the instruments in the autoclave for sterilization.
Hospitals rely on autoclaves for the sterilization of medical supplies.
The veterinary clinic used an autoclave for animal surgery tools.
The beauty salon used an autoclave for safe piercing equipment sterilization.

senses.definition: an instrument used by physicians or scientists in medical operations for picking up, holding, and pulling things
a small thin-bladed surgical knife particularly used in surgery
a piece of medical equipment that facilitates breathing by sending air in and out of the lungs to assist patients in cases of respiratory failure
a medical device that generates heat in tissues using high-frequency electrical currents for therapeutic purposes

examples.x: The therapist used a diathermy machine for muscle pain.
Blood circulation improved with the diathermy machine for healing.
In physical therapy, joints receive targeted care through the diathermy machine.
Doctors may recommend the diathermy machine for arthritis pain management.
The diathermy machine proves to be a simple and effective non-invasive therapy.

senses.definition: a medical device that measures blood pressure by applying and releasing external pressure to the artery, providing systolic and diastolic readings

examples.x: The nurse used a sphygmomanometer to check my blood pressure.
Doctors use a sphygmomanometer for routine health check-ups.
During a medical examination, the doctor employed a sphygmomanometer.
Home blood pressure monitoring kits often include a sphygmomanometer.

senses.definition: a medical device used to measure blood pressure with an inflatable cuff, gauge, and stethoscope or digital display
a device that measures and analyzes body composition for health and fitness assessment
a portable medical device used for 24-hour heart activity monitoring to assess heart rhythm and detect cardiac abnormalities
a medical device for precise tissue cutting, coagulation, and cauterization during surgery, using electrical energy
a backlit panel for viewing x-ray films and radiographic images by healthcare professionals
a medical tool for examining the interior of the eye, aiding in the diagnosis of eye conditions

examples.x: During the checkup, the nurse examined my eyes with an ophthalmoscope.
Eye specialists use ophthalmoscopes to detect diseases like glaucoma.
The eye doctor used an ophthalmoscope to check my retinas.
During an eye exam, the ophthalmoscope revealed details of my optic nerve.
The pediatrician used a child-friendly ophthalmoscope for my kid's eyes.

senses.definition: a device used during surgery to remove smoke and odors from electrocautery or laser devices for a clear and safe surgical field
a medical instrument used to assess reflexes by striking tendons during neurological examinations
an electronic device implanted inside a person's body to help regulate their heart rate
a slender medical instrument with a blunt end used particularly for exploration of wounds or body cavities
a medical instrument used during the internal genitalia examination to dilate the opening of a body cavity

examples.x: During the pelvic exam, the gynecologist gently inserted a speculum into the vagina to visualize the cervix and vaginal walls.
The nurse assisted the doctor in positioning the patient for the speculum examination, ensuring comfort and proper visualization.
The speculum was lubricated before insertion to minimize discomfort and facilitate smooth entry.
The gynecologist carefully adjusted the speculum to obtain a clear view of the vaginal canal and cervix.
Patients undergoing a speculum examination are often advised to relax and breathe deeply to ease any discomfort during the procedure.

senses.definition: an instrument fitted with two poles and a long piece of canvas used to carry the sick, the injured, or the dead
a hypodermic device used in emergencies to treat a  severe allergic reaction by giving an injection of epinephrine
an immobilizing device, such as a cast or brace, that protects a broken or injured part of the body during therapy or recovery period
a device that keeps someone alive when they are seriously ill and are unable to breathe on their own
an electronic device that warns of trouble and continuously tracks the operation of a system
a breathing device worn over the nose and mouth that is hooked up to an oxygen tank
a slender, solid, often sharp-pointed instrument used for withdrawing blood samples, injecting medicine, etc.

examples.x: The nurse used a needle to administer the vaccine.
He felt a slight pinch as the needle entered his arm.
The acupuncturist inserted a tiny needle to relieve his back pain.
The syringe and needle were disposed of in a medical waste container.
They developed a new type of needle that reduces pain during injections.

senses.definition: a tube with a long hollow needle at the end that is used to inject or withdraw fluids
a device used to weigh people or objects

examples.x: The bathroom scale indicated that she had lost a few pounds after her diet and exercise regimen.
The warehouse manager used a large industrial scale to weigh the shipments of goods.
The doctor's office had a digital scale to measure patients' weight accurately during check-ups.
The airline baggage check-in counter had a scale to ensure that luggage met weight restrictions.
The jeweler employed a precision scale to weigh precious metals and gemstones for crafting jewelry.

senses.definition: a metal enclosing framework with wheels and handles that helps disabled or frail people maintain their balance while walking
specialized cutting instruments used in surgery for precise cutting and dissecting of tissues with serrated edges and different blade shapes
a magnifying tool used in surgery for precise visualization of the surgical field

examples.x: The surgeon used an operating microscope for delicate eye surgery.
Scientists employ an operating microscope to study tiny organisms.
Dental procedures often require the use of an operating microscope.
An operating microscope aids in precision during neurosurgery.
Microsurgery relies on an operating microscope for intricate procedures.

senses.definition: a stitch or series of stitches made to secure the edges of a wound or surgical opening

examples.x: The surgeon skillfully applied a suture to close the incision after the successful appendectomy.
Following the dental procedure, the oral surgeon used a suture to close the gum tissue around the extracted tooth.
The plastic surgeon carefully placed sutures to minimize scarring after the cosmetic surgery.
The athlete received several stitches as the sports medicine physician applied a suture to the deep cut on the knee.

senses.definition: a surgical instrument used to hold and manipulate needles during suturing procedures
a surgical tool for holding tissues aside during procedures

examples.x: The dentist used a retractor to keep my cheek out of the way.
During surgery, the retractor held back the skin for better access.
A retractor helped the surgeon see the underlying organs clearly.
The nurse assisted by positioning the retractor for optimal visibility.
The retractor gently held the tissue aside during the stitching.

senses.definition: the tools or methods that repair or enhance physiological functions for health improvement

examples.x: Dental crowns serve as restoratives for damaged teeth.
Hearing aids are common restoratives for hearing loss.
Artificial joints are essential restoratives in orthopedic surgery.

senses.definition: a small electronic device that is worn in or behind the ear and is specifically designed to amplify inaudible sounds to help a person with hearing loss
a medical instrument used in auscultation for detecting sounds generated inside the body, such as heartbeat and breathing

examples.x: The doctor used a stethoscope to listen to the patient's heartbeat and lung sounds during the physical examination.
The nurse hung her stethoscope around her neck as she prepared to conduct rounds on the hospital wards.
The cardiologist relied on the stethoscope to detect murmurs and irregularities in the patient's heart rhythm.
Medical students are trained to use the stethoscope to auscultate various body sounds, including heart, lung, and bowel sounds.
The veterinarian used a specialized stethoscope designed for animals to assess the health of the dog's heart and lungs.
